metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1258
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig. 4A: Surface GluA1 staining, but not total GluA1, in PV-positive interneurons was lower in erbin KO mice compared with wild-type controls.
Fig. 4D, E: The amplitude of peak AMPA currents in fast-spiking interneurons were reduced after puff application of AMPA. Together, this supports the notion that erbin regulates the surface insertion, not just synaptic targeting, of AMPA receptors.. Experimental description: erbin-/- and erbinΔC/ΔC mice were described previously27. Gad67-GFP mice were from Y. Yanagawa28. Mice (five in a cage) were housed in a room with a 12-h light/dark cycle with access to food and water ad libitum. Animal experiments were approved by the institutional animal care and use committee of the Georgia Regents University. Unless otherwise indicated, control mice are wild-type littermates.
